why: Cross-iteration regression detector for v1 of the skill. Every iteration scores against this set in addition to live evals and to all earlier frozen cohorts (frozen-v1, ..., v(N-1)). If live scores improve but a frozen cohort drops, the iteration overfit. Snapshotted by scripts/snapshot_frozen.py — first N trigger and first M outcome entries from the live set at snapshot time.

trigger_evals.query: Find me senior backend engineer roles in Seattle, $180k+
trigger_evals.rationale: Explicit job-search request with role, location, and compensation - the canonical trigger case from the description.
Resume-to-job matching - direct quoted phrase from the description's trigger list.

trigger_evals.query: Look for openings near me, I'm a marketing manager
trigger_evals.rationale: Location-based search with role context. 'look for openings' is in the description trigger phrases.

trigger_evals.query: Help me apply to this posting: https://boards.greenhouse.io/example/jobs/12345
trigger_evals.rationale: Application help with a real posting URL - exercises Phase 3 tailoring path.

trigger_evals.query: Search local jobs in Austin for a data analyst
trigger_evals.rationale: 'Search local jobs' is a quoted trigger phrase; exercises Tier 3 local sources path.

outcome_evals.prompt: Find me senior backend engineer roles in Austin, $180k+. Here's my resume: [attaches resume.pdf with 8 years Python/Go backend experience, AWS, distributed systems, Stripe + ex-Google]
outcome_evals.expected_output: Phase 1 is satisfied (role/location/comp all provided); skill proceeds to Phase 2 search. Output is an HTML file in the workspace with 10+ unique postings across Tier 1 (LinkedIn/Indeed/Glassdoor/ZipRecruiter), Tier 2 (Dice/Built In/HN Who's Hiring), Tier 3 (Texas Workforce Commission/Austin Business Journal/local boards), and Tier 4 (career pages of major Austin employers). Each posting has match strength (Strong/Good/Possible), source attribution, and clickable links.

assertions.text: Skill does NOT re-ask for role, location, or comp - Phase 1 gating is satisfied by the prompt
Search covers at least 3 of the 4 tiers (major boards / niche / local / company pages)
Output is delivered as an HTML file saved to the workspace folder, not bare URLs in chat
Each posting includes: title, company, blurb, location, salary (if listed), date, source, summary, match strength, URL
Match strength uses the 80%+/50-80%/<50% rubric, not invented categories
Includes 'Sources searched' summary at the bottom (transparency)
